title: How to Do a Personal Breakthrough Session
source: NLP Supremacy Master Time Line Therapy Practitioner Training

preSession.title: Before the Session
screeningQuestions: For how long have you had this problem? What have you done about it? Did the things that you previously did, work? (If an MD, Psychologist, or Psychiatrist then ask for referral.)
How intense are your feelings which are associated with the problem (on a scale from 1-10)?
Can you get in touch with the emotions as we talk?
Do you ever feel positive/negative emotions?
Have you heard that you have an Unconscious Mind?
Why do you want to let go of the problem?
Are you at Cause for your creation of this problem? (explain)
How will you know that the problem has disappeared at the end of our session(s)? What will you see, hear, feel, say to yourself when the problem has disappeared?
What will happen when you get your outcome? What will you feel? How will your family (business associates) react to you letting go of your problem?
We may give you some Tasking before we see you. You must do those tasks or we cannot see you. Do you understand? Is that OK?
Referral needed (currently seeing other health care professional)?
preSession.complementaryApproachDisclosure: Ask, 'Do you understand that this is a complementary approach?' That means that I am a legal Complementary Healthcare Provider, and not a licensed Medical Doctor, Psychologist, Psychiatrist, Master's in Therapy, or a Master's in Social Work. The self-regulated holistic treatments and client-centered disciplines that I am trained in and in which I have experience doing include, consulting and coaching, Time Line Therapy techniques, Neuro Linguistic Programming (NLP) and Hypnosis.

ordealAssignment.title: You may need to assign an ordeal
whenToAssign: When the client is not paying for it himself
When he has seen multiple therapists for the problem
When he feels the problem is significant ('My problem is different or bigger than anyone else's.')
When he has a sense of pride or identity in having the problem
characteristics: Get agreement that he will do it before you assign it
The ordeal should symbolically represent the source of problem, and it should cause the problem to unravel
It should cause the client to make an investment of energy and attention, to do what you say, bringing him into compliance with your instructions
It should bring into consciousness certain areas of thinking or behavior that were out of consciousness (EG: Food journals for weight loss.)
It could include what the client has absolutely avoided or denied that created the problem - what is it that they MOST do not want to do (which could be related to the root cause)

detailedPersonalHistory.title: Detailed Personal History Questions
detailedPersonalHistory.instruction: As you ask these, note if the answers suggest the client is at cause or effect. If at effect, then who or what is the authority or the cause?

questions.question: Why are you here? Why else? Why else? Etc.
questions.purpose: Elicit all reasons for client being here.

questions.question: How do you know you have this problem? How is that a problem? How do you do it? When do you do (not do) it? Can you do the problem now?
questions.purpose: Elicits a reality strategy for problem and any diagnoses made.

questions.question: How long have you had it? Was there ever a time when you didn't? What have you done about it?
questions.purpose: Establishes timeline and previous attempts at resolution.

questions.question: What happened the first time you had this? What emotions were present?
questions.purpose: Identifies the initial sensitizing event and associated emotions.

questions.question: What events have happened since then? What emotions were present?
questions.purpose: Maps the chain of significant emotional events.

questions.question: In each of these events, what is the relationship between the event and your current situation in life?
questions.purpose: Connects past events to present symptomology.

questions.question: Tell me about your parents, brothers, sisters, etc. What is the relationship between this person (mother, father, etc.) and your current situation?
questions.purpose: Identifies family dynamics and modeling influences.

questions.question: Tell me about your childhood in relationship to this problem.
questions.purpose: Explores early life context for the problem.

questions.question: Is there a purpose for this problem? Is there a reason for having the problem? Ask your unconscious mind.
questions.purpose: Checks for secondary gain and unconscious purpose.

questions.question: When did you choose to have this situation be created? Why? Ask your unconscious mind.
questions.purpose: Identifies the decision point and unconscious motivation.

questions.question: Is there anything your unconscious mind wants you to know, or is there anything you're not getting which, if you got it, would allow the problem to disappear?
questions.purpose: Accesses unconscious resources and missing information.

questions.question: Is it OK with your unconscious mind to support us in removing this problem today, and for it to allow you to have an undeniable experience of it, when we are complete?
questions.purpose: Gets unconscious agreement and buy-in for the process.

questions.question: How will you know when this problem has totally disappeared? (Get the client's evidence procedure.) So, when we get rid of ___, will the presenting problem totally disappear?
questions.purpose: Establishes the evidence procedure for success.

additionalQuestions: What do you need to do that you don't want to do?
What do you not want to do that you must?
What is it about this problem that you cannot tell anyone?
What is the relationship between this problem and problems you have in other areas of your life?
Will any of those problems stop you from solving this problem?

How will you know specifically that the problem is gone? Restate it: 'So, this is how you will know that the problem is gone?' Listen for binds or double binds at this point as well as at the end which could negate the effects of the process.
detailedPersonalHistory.causeAndEffect: When did they choose to have these symptoms? Ask why did the Unconscious Mind choose to create this? Check for secondary gain.
detailedPersonalHistory.models: Who did they model in youth? Connecting health / power / protection? Find out client's self-image - who did they model their life on? (The client may have extreme external frame of reference.)

setFrames.title: Setting Frames for the Session
setFrames.description: Before beginning the intervention, establish the frames that will support the client's change process.

frames.title: Beliefs to Adopt
frames.description: Any beliefs you want the client to adopt before beginning the intervention work.

frames.title: Why Time Line Therapy Techniques Work
frames.description: Explain the basis of Time Line Therapy techniques and why emotions release.

frames.title: Cause and Effect
frames.description: Ensure the client understands and accepts being at cause for their results.

frames.title: Getting Value from the Process
frames.description: Frame the importance of being fully engaged and committed to the process.

frames.title: The Importance of Taking Action
frames.description: Emphasize that change requires action and focus after the session.

frames.title: Task Agreement
frames.description: Remind the client that there will be a Task after the session. Get their agreement for doing it.

elicitValues.title: Elicit Values
elicitValues.description: Elicit the client's values in the area of life in which the problem falls. (In healing, 'What's important about LIVING,' not just health.) Evaluate the client's values. Make sure that they are appropriate for the solution of the problem.

willingnessToPlay.title: Determine - is the client willing to play at 100%
willingnessToPlay.description: Ask what they are willing to do or to be to have the problem disappear. Are they willing to do ANYTHING? What are they not willing to do? Also ask Unconscious Mind what they need to do: may need to give up current lifestyle.

setOutcomes.title: Set Outcomes
setOutcomes.description: Do standard outcome setting, as in Practitioner Time Line Therapy Training.

threeRequisites.title: Three Requisites for Change
requisites: Getting rid of Negative Emotions, Limiting Decisions, Parts Integration, Values, Create the Future
Focusing on What You Want (Your Evaluations Become Suggestions to the Unconscious Mind)

preIntervention.title: Elicit Values
preIntervention.description: Elicit the client's values in the area of life in which the problem falls. (In healing, 'What's important about LIVING,' not just health.) Evaluate the client's values. Make sure that they are appropriate for the solution of the problem.

preIntervention.title: Determine - is the client willing to play at 100%
preIntervention.description: Ask what they are willing to do or to be to have the problem disappear. Are they willing to do ANYTHING? What are they not willing to do? Also ask Unconscious Mind what they need to do: may need to give up current lifestyle. (This issue may be the most important, because if the intervention goes perfectly, if the client does not make specific changes in behavior, circumstance or situation it still may not work.)

preIntervention.title: Set Outcomes
preIntervention.description: Do standard outcome setting, as in Practitioner Time Line Therapy Training.

preIntervention.title: Set up the Frames for coaching
preIntervention.description: Any beliefs you want the client to adopt, why Time Line Therapy techniques work, why emotions release, cause and effect, getting value from the process, the importance of taking action and focus. Remind the client that there will be a Task after the session. Get their agreement for doing it.

preIntervention.title: Tell Client about the three requisites for change
preIntervention.description: Getting rid of Negative Emotions, Limiting Decisions, Parts Integration, Values, Create the Future. Taking Action (Includes Enforcing your Boundaries). Focusing on What You Want (Your Evaluations Become Suggestions to the Unconscious Mind).
requisites: Getting rid of Negative Emotions, Limiting Decisions, Parts Integration, Values, Create the Future
Focusing on What You Want (Your Evaluations Become Suggestions to the Unconscious Mind)

intervention.title: Pain Paradigm
intervention.description: If you are doing Pain Paradigm, do it here and then take a week's break (minimum).

intervention.title: Start with Negative Emotions
intervention.description: Get rid of Anger, Sadness, Fear, Hurt, Guilt and any others that the client has mentioned during the History. (This is also the order in which to do the emotions.)
notes: Do all Negative Emotions in the case of getting rid of away-froms, and in healing.
Do only the Negative Emotions the client mentions in all other cases

intervention.title: Find out if the Greater Problem is gone
intervention.description: If not, eliminate it by getting rid of the related limiting decision.

intervention.title: Eliminate all Limiting Decisions
intervention.description: Any limiting decisions mentioned during the History as well as anywhere the client is not at cause for any limiting decisions or emotions. Also, the Limiting Decision to create problem or the condition. (If eliminating away-froms in Values Hierarchy, then use this step to get rid of all other away-froms)

intervention.title: Do Parts Integration
intervention.description: Even if the issue of parts did not come up or even if there were no obvious conflicts, you should check on the possibility of parts.

intervention.title: Review all your written material
intervention.description: Go through your notes. Make sure that you have handled everything the client mentioned during the History, etc. Check with client. Get agreement that you have handled everything.

intervention.title: Test all negative emotions
intervention.description: Make sure they are gone. The Presenting Problem should also be gone.

intervention.title: Remind client of evidence procedure, have him check
intervention.description: For example, if the issue was a behavior, test and make sure that he cannot run it.

intervention.title: Go back and release anything else you have found in testing
intervention.description: I.E. do anything else based on problems and examples (parts, chaining anchors, negative emotions, Limiting Decisions, Prime Concerns, Strategies etc.) Re-Elicit Values: Make sure they are appropriate for the change. Change / modify the values hierarchy if necessary. Re-adjust the values hierarchy to maintain the same motivation as was previously provided by any away-from's removed.

intervention.title: Check Ecology and/or Conflicts
intervention.description: 'Is there any reason why it would not be OK for this change to continue for the future?' Do Parts Integrations to integrate all conflicts (in healing make sure that you integrate conflict between problem and immune system. E.G. Integrate the virus and the immune system).

intervention.title: Hypnosis/Pendulum
intervention.description: Enlist the Unconscious Mind's assistance in healing the body, or eliminating the problem. Set dates for success. Get agreement. (In AIDS: Use pendulum to increase the TS-cells-the suppressors.) If appropriate, change chemistry of immune function while healing. Do Hypnosis: If needed and if appropriate.

intervention.title: Future Pace
intervention.description: When the client goes out on the Time Line, the problem should be gone for 1 year, 5 years, 10 years, etc. Often people at home do not believe the client is any better, so you have to do a massive future pace and install a recovery strategy for doubting Thomas's at home.

intervention.title: Again, remind client of evidence procedure, have him check
intervention.description: For example, if the presenting problem was a behavior, test and make sure that he cannot do it. Get agreement from client that you are done.

postIntervention.title: Set Future Goals (CYF Process)
postIntervention.description: Put the goals in the future.

completingSession.title: Completing the Session

steps.title: Remind the Client of the three requisites for change
requisites: Getting rid of Negative Emotions, Limiting Decisions, Parts Integration, Values, Create the Future
Focusing on What You Want (Your Evaluations Become Suggestions to the Unconscious Mind)

steps.title: Assign any Tasks that are appropriate
steps.description: As often as possible and when necessary, assign task(s) that the client needs. The tasks should presuppose and put the client in charge of maintaining success, should cause the client to take action, focus on what he wants, and play at 100%. They should be assigned as a requirement (remember the client has already agreed to do them), and should reinforce the changes made, I.E. self-hypnosis, journaling, meditation, exercise, pendulum, etc. Assigned Tasks should, wherever possible, continue the relationship with the Unconscious Mind. Be sure to include: whatever the client describes as being necessary for change.

steps.title: Schedule any follow-up phone calls or sessions

completingSession.later: Follow-up, make sure the client communicated with you.
